title: Metrics panel layout: summary counts and chart area
description:
Define the layout of the metrics panel. When space allows (wide enough viewport), it shows three conceptual columns: a narrow left column for summary counts and two wider columns for the chart. On narrower viewports the layout collapses gracefully.

Scenario: Metrics panel matches board visual style

Given the metrics view is active
Then the metrics panel uses the same flat card styling as the kanban board columns
And the same background color, border treatment, rounded corners, and color tokens
And no overlay or shadow that would make it appear to float above the board

Scenario: Left column shows total count prominently

Given the metrics view is active
Then the left column displays a large prominent number representing the total count of issues in the current filtered view
And the number updates when active filters change

Scenario: Left column shows breakdown by status

Given the metrics view is active
Then below the total count there is a breakdown showing count per status
And each row shows the status name and its count

Scenario: Left column shows breakdown by project when virtual projects exist

Given the user has virtual projects configured
And the metrics view is active
Then the left column also shows a count breakdown by project

Scenario: Left column shows breakdown by scope when local issues exist

Given the user has local issues
And the metrics view is active
Then the left column also shows a count breakdown by scope (project vs local)

Scenario: Right two-thirds shows the chart area

Given the metrics view is active on a wide enough viewport
Then the right portion (approximately two-thirds of the panel width) is reserved for the stacked bar chart
And the chart fills its container responsively
creator: ryan.porter
